message:
fix(readiness): index discovered connectors by name, not description, for free-text search

`DiscoveredConnector::new` builds three lowercase strings used by the
free-text search index over discovered connectors: `search_slug_lower`,
`search_name_lower`, and `search_cohort_lower`. The middle field was
incorrectly populated from `summary.description` instead of
`summary.name`, so:

* searching by the connector's *name* missed every match (the actual
  name was never indexed under `name_lower`),
* the field was effectively a duplicate of whatever description text
  was already searchable elsewhere, and
* per-connector relevance ranking that combined slug+name+cohort was
  mis-weighted because the "name" axis was carrying long-form prose.

Switch to `summary.name.to_lowercase()` so `search_name_lower` actually
holds the connector's name. One-line fix; no test churn here because
the existing indexer tests asserted only that *some* lowercased field
matched — those will now match the correct one.
